selfcheck.chosen_text: Evitar duplicar el mismo par en orden invertido
selfcheck.note: Theory CHECK(entity_a < entity_b) canonical pair order.

selfcheck.chosen_text: Nueva fila por cambio de decisión
selfcheck.note: Theory: no overwrite prior decision; insert new versioned row.

selfcheck.chosen_text: Ser atómicas en la misma transacción lógica
selfcheck.note: Theory ACID: decisión + evidencia commit together or not.

selfcheck.chosen_text: Encapsula acceso a datos y facilita tests con :memory:
selfcheck.note: Theory repository pattern for data access + testability.

exercises.code:
import sqlite3
c = sqlite3.connect(':memory:')
c.execute('CREATE TABLE entities(id TEXT PRIMARY KEY)')
c.execute("INSERT INTO entities VALUES ('e1')")
print(c.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM entities').fetchone()[0])
exercises.note: Theory entities table pattern.

exercises.code:
import sqlite3
c=sqlite3.connect(':memory:')
c.execute('create table p(score real check(score between 0 and 1))')
try:
    c.execute('insert into p values (1.5)')
except sqlite3.IntegrityError:
    print('bad_score')
exercises.note: Theory CHECK score 0..1; IntegrityError is sqlite name used in starter ecosystem.
Theory CHECK entity_a < entity_b.

exercises.code:
import sqlite3
c=sqlite3.connect(':memory:')
c.execute('create table d(pair text, label text)')
c.execute("insert into d values ('p1','match')")
c.execute("insert into d values ('p1','non_match')")
print(c.execute('select count(*) from d').fetchone()[0])
exercises.note: Theory append-only decisions: new row per change.
Theory provenance source_system / source_record_id.
Theory valid_from/valid_to temporalidad.

exercises.code:
import sqlite3
c=sqlite3.connect(':memory:')
c.executescript('create table pairs(id text); create table dec(pair_id text); insert into pairs values ("p1"),("p2"); insert into dec values ("p1");')
rows = c.execute('select id from pairs where id not in (select pair_id from dec)').fetchall()
print([r[0] for r in rows])
exercises.note: Theory anti-join pairs without decision.
Theory windows rank scores; sorted desc top.
Instruction names CTE ranked; theory WITH names steps for ranked scores.
S28 cardinality + theory n×m explosion; combinatoria de pares.
Theory NULL: use IS NULL; Python is None.
Theory EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN scans vs search; instruction default SCAN.

exercises.code:
import sqlite3
c=sqlite3.connect(':memory:')
c.execute('create table t(x int)')
c.execute('BEGIN')
c.execute('insert into t values (1)')
c.execute('ROLLBACK')
print(c.execute('select count(*) from t').fetchone()[0])
exercises.note: Theory: demonstrate rollback if second insert fails.
Theory ACID letters.
Theory decisión+evidencia atomic; abort if evidence fails.

exercises.code:
import sqlite3
c=sqlite3.connect(':memory:')
c.execute('create table e(id text primary key, name text)')
c.execute("insert into e values ('1','A')")
c.execute("insert into e values ('1','B') on conflict(id) do update set name=excluded.name")
print(c.execute("select name from e where id='1'").fetchone()[0])
exercises.note: Theory upsert INSERT…ON CONFLICT; SQL form completed from taught keywords + starter.
Theory mark jobs pending after crash.
Theory constraints + reintento on duplicate pair.

exercises.code:
import sqlite3
c=sqlite3.connect(':memory:')
c.execute('create table schema_migrations(v int primary key, name text)')
c.execute("insert into schema_migrations values (1,'init')")
c.execute("insert into schema_migrations values (2,'add_index')")
print(c.execute('select max(v) from schema_migrations').fetchone()[0])
exercises.note: Theory schema_migrations table.
Theory indexes on block_key; instruction suggested name.
Theory avoid destructive drops without backup.
Theory repository encapsulates access.
Theory pooling mentioned in outcomes/topic; conceptual pool_size.
Theory repo tests with :memory:.
